retrieved.title: Closing a degree programme/Withdrawing from the University of Vienna
retrieved.text:
You can end your studies in the following ways:

- successfully completing your degree programme and graduating
- closing one or multiple degree programme(s) (formerly called "exmatriculation")

Closing degree programmes is possible all year round. This is how you proceed:

1. Deregister from all courses with continous assessment and examinations. This is possible via u:space during the deregistration period. Afterwards, please contact the lecturers directly. Closing your studies does not qualify as an important reason for the cancellation of an examination (info only available in German).
2. In case, return books to the University Library or institute libraries. You can only withdraw (= close all degree programmes at the University of Vienna) if you have not borrowed any books.
3. If you close a bachelor-/diploma or master programme, please message us via the [Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/category/stuz) and specify which degree programme you want to close. If you want to close a doctoral/PhD programme, please contact [doktorat.zulassung@univie.ac.at](javascript:linkTo_UnCryptMailto('ocknvq,fqmvqtcv0bwncuuwpiBwpkxkg0ce0cv');).
4. After a few days, we will inform you about the processing of your application. Check the changed status of your degree programme in u:space at Studies > Study overview. You can download your confirmation of withdrawal at Personal matters > My documents.

- My secondary-school leaving certificate is from an EU/EEA country, but I have citizenship of a non-EU/EEA country. How can I change my degree programme

  Submit your application for admission within the application period for non-EU/EEA citizens. Follow the steps for changing your degree programme with secondary-school leaving certificate and citizenship from an EU/EEA country. In this way, your change of degree programme can be conducted earlier and faster – and you can start your new degree programme in a less stressful way.

  You can prove that you belong to a group of persons according to the [Decree on Groups of Persons](/en/non-eueea-nationals-with-equivalent-status/)? If this is the case, you can also submit your application within the application period for EU/EEA citizens. Submit the proof of belonging to a group of persons. The change of degree programme will take longer, as your application must be processed individually, and you have to wait for your admission letter. With this letter you will also receive the info you need to complete the change of degree programme.

retrieved.text: Entrance exam procedures only take place once per academic year (before the winter semester). The entrance exam procedure for bachelor and diploma programmes is only valid for the winter and summer semester following the procedure. The successfully completed entrance exam procedure for the bachelor programme in Teacher Education it is valid indefinitely. 1. Complete the [Online-Self-Assessment](http://self-assessment.univie.ac.at) (OSA) of the desired degree programme. Afterwards you receive your OSA code, which you will need in the further procedure. 2. Apply for admission to the degree programme you would like to change to within the application period. Choose the desired degree programme in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en) > Studies > Apply for admission and upload the [required documents](/en/entrance-exam/). Documents that were already confirmed by the Admission Office (status "confirmed") do not need to be uploaded again. Enter your OSA code. Check the status of your application in u:space under "My application". This status has to be "application submitted". Only then you are registered for the entrance exam procedure. 3. Participate in the [entrance exam](/en/entrance-exam/). 4. An admission to the degree programme is only possible after you have successfully participated in the entrance exam procedure. retrieved.title: Change of bachelor/diploma programmes
retrieved.text:
1. Complete the [Online-Self-Assessment](http://self-assessment.univie.ac.at) (OSA) of the desired degree programme. Afterwards you receive your OSA code, which you will need in the further procedure.
2. Apply for admission to the degree programme you would like to change to within the application period. Choose the desired degree programme in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en) > Studies > Apply for admission and upload the [required documents](/en/admission-procedure/bachelordiploma-programmes/). Documents that were already confirmed by the Admission Office (status "confirmed") do not need to be uploaded again. Enter your OSA code.
   Check the status of your application in u:space under "My application". This status has to be "application submitted".
3. You can be admitted to the new degree programme if you fulfil all requirements. You will receive an admission letter to your u:account e-mail address after a processing time of few weeks ([webmail.univie.ac.at](https://www.univie.ac.at/ZID/webmail/index.html?lang=en)). You can also find the admission letter in u:space > Personal matters > My documents.
4. Message us via the [Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/62/create/936) after you have received your admission letter. Indicate which degree programme you would like to close. The Admission Office will effectuate the change of degree programme, which you will see in u:space > Study overview after a few working days. You will also receive a confirmation via e-mail.

- I want to take a module examination from an extension curriculum (EC) to fulfil the QZB. How should I proceed?

  In the non-degree programme you are only admitted to "attending individual courses". Therefore, you cannot register for the extension curriculum as such. If you have any questions regarding the registration for examinations, please contact the [StudiesServiceCenter or StudiesServiceUnit](https://www.univie.ac.at/en/about-us/governance-structure/organisation-of-studies-courses/) of your degree programme.

  Please contact the [StudiesServiceCenter or StudiesServiceUnit](https://www.univie.ac.at/en/about-us/governance-structure/organisation-of-studies-courses/) of the degree programme in which the course is offered.
- How can I switch to the degree programme?

  You have successfully completed all courses and fulfil the required qualitative admission requirements in full? Apply for admission to the desired master programme in u:space within the application period. Your application will be processed according to the prevailing legal status at that time.

reranked.title: Closing a degree programme/Withdrawing from the University of Vienna
reranked.text:
You can end your studies in the following ways:

- successfully completing your degree programme and graduating
- closing one or multiple degree programme(s) (formerly called "exmatriculation")

Closing degree programmes is possible all year round. This is how you proceed:

1. Deregister from all courses with continous assessment and examinations. This is possible via u:space during the deregistration period. Afterwards, please contact the lecturers directly. Closing your studies does not qualify as an important reason for the cancellation of an examination (info only available in German).
2. In case, return books to the University Library or institute libraries. You can only withdraw (= close all degree programmes at the University of Vienna) if you have not borrowed any books.
3. If you close a bachelor-/diploma or master programme, please message us via the [Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/category/stuz) and specify which degree programme you want to close. If you want to close a doctoral/PhD programme, please contact [doktorat.zulassung@univie.ac.at](javascript:linkTo_UnCryptMailto('ocknvq,fqmvqtcv0bwncuuwpiBwpkxkg0ce0cv');).
4. After a few days, we will inform you about the processing of your application. Check the changed status of your degree programme in u:space at Studies > Study overview. You can download your confirmation of withdrawal at Personal matters > My documents.

reranked.text:
/

- My secondary-school leaving certificate is from an EU/EEA country, but I have citizenship of a non-EU/EEA country. How can I change my degree programme

  Submit your application for admission within the application period for non-EU/EEA citizens. Follow the steps for changing your degree programme with secondary-school leaving certificate and citizenship from an EU/EEA country. In this way, your change of degree programme can be conducted earlier and faster – and you can start your new degree programme in a less stressful way.

  You can prove that you belong to a group of persons according to the [Decree on Groups of Persons](/en/non-eueea-nationals-with-equivalent-status/)? If this is the case, you can also submit your application within the application period for EU/EEA citizens. Submit the proof of belonging to a group of persons. The change of degree programme will take longer, as your application must be processed individually, and you have to wait for your admission letter. With this letter you will also receive the info you need to complete the change of degree programme.

  In the non-degree programme you are only admitted to "attending individual courses". Therefore, you cannot register for the extension curriculum as such. selected.title: Closing a degree programme/Withdrawing from the University of Vienna
selected.text:
You can end your studies in the following ways:

- successfully completing your degree programme and graduating
- closing one or multiple degree programme(s) (formerly called "exmatriculation")

Closing degree programmes is possible all year round. This is how you proceed:

1. Deregister from all courses with continous assessment and examinations. This is possible via u:space during the deregistration period. Afterwards, please contact the lecturers directly. Closing your studies does not qualify as an important reason for the cancellation of an examination (info only available in German).
2. In case, return books to the University Library or institute libraries. You can only withdraw (= close all degree programmes at the University of Vienna) if you have not borrowed any books.
3. If you close a bachelor-/diploma or master programme, please message us via the [Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/category/stuz) and specify which degree programme you want to close. If you want to close a doctoral/PhD programme, please contact [doktorat.zulassung@univie.ac.at](javascript:linkTo_UnCryptMailto('ocknvq,fqmvqtcv0bwncuuwpiBwpkxkg0ce0cv');).
4. After a few days, we will inform you about the processing of your application. Check the changed status of your degree programme in u:space at Studies > Study overview. You can download your confirmation of withdrawal at Personal matters > My documents.

selected.text:

- My secondary-school leaving certificate is from an EU/EEA country, but I have citizenship of a non-EU/EEA country. How can I change my degree programme

  Submit your application for admission within the application period for non-EU/EEA citizens. Follow the steps for changing your degree programme with secondary-school leaving certificate and citizenship from an EU/EEA country. In this way, your change of degree programme can be conducted earlier and faster – and you can start your new degree programme in a less stressful way.

  You can prove that you belong to a group of persons according to the [Decree on Groups of Persons](/en/non-eueea-nationals-with-equivalent-status/)? If this is the case, you can also submit your application within the application period for EU/EEA citizens. Submit the proof of belonging to a group of persons. The change of degree programme will take longer, as your application must be processed individually, and you have to wait for your admission letter. With this letter you will also receive the info you need to complete the change of degree programme.

[1] ABC of terminology — S
The University of Vienna conducts selection procedures for some master programmes. This mainly applies to English-language programmes. For some master programmes, you must provide proof of qualitative admission requirements ("qualitative Zulassungsbedingungen") and/or upload a letter of motivation when applying for admission. These programmes have their own application deadlines (always in spring, for the following academic year). Related links: - [Master programmes with selection procedure](/en/admission-procedure/master-programmes/#c876069) Related entries: - [Application period](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=30198&cHash=83dcf3f2a3a1fe41d6eef2f91f47ba7c) - Semester abroad As a degree student you can pass one or more semesters abroad at a different university (Outgoing Mobility). The University of Vienna cooperates with numerous partner universities abroad. There are also several mobility programmes offered for incoming students. Related Links: - [International Office](https://international.univie.ac.at/en/) Related entries: - [CEEPUS](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=29987&cHash=72c1e3c31b64c2842340511847949735) - [Free Mover](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=29995&cHash=5488b37118f3efe558bb850a9466ab5d) - [Recognition of exams](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=30187&cHash=7ad03787bb35116164cf00dbe2cdd76a) - [International Office](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=30849&cHash=6aab915f8821fb9ddb9f2a36c1d8438f) - [Erasmus+](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=30864&cHash=ea57c8821cd2999c69dcc17641651743) - [Mobility programmes](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31188&cHash=813707a12daceba2774512659b917d60) - [Visiting Master](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31337&cHash=70e303dffac1661c3aafc82b37c76b70) - Seminar (course type) Related entries: - [Course with continuous assessment (pi LV)](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31243&cHash=04d9e48aefae1365779d07a302248e56) - Senate The Senate consists of 18 members. Together with the Rectorate and the University Board, it forms the governing body of the University of Vienna. Every three years, the Senate is elected by the academic and non-academic university staff. Related Links: - [Senate of the University of Vienna (in German)](https://senat.univie.ac.at/infos-zum-senat/) Related entries: - [Statutes](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31252&cHash=9dc01c87dda397fab5d23352329e6a42) - Special libraries The special libraries belong to the University Library, however, they are located in the different institutes. There you can find literature related

[2] Closing a degree programme/Withdrawing from the University of Vienna
You can end your studies in the following ways:

- successfully completing your degree programme and graduating
- closing one or multiple degree programme(s) (formerly called "exmatriculation")

Closing degree programmes is possible all year round. This is how you proceed:

1. Deregister from all courses with continous assessment and examinations. This is possible via u:space during the deregistration period. Afterwards, please contact the lecturers directly. Closing your studies does not qualify as an important reason for the cancellation of an examination (info only available in German).
2. In case, return books to the University Library or institute libraries. You can only withdraw (= close all degree programmes at the University of Vienna) if you have not borrowed any books.
3. If you close a bachelor-/diploma or master programme, please message us via the [Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/category/stuz) and specify which degree programme you want to close. If you want to close a doctoral/PhD programme, please contact [doktorat.zulassung@univie.ac.at](javascript:linkTo_UnCryptMailto('ocknvq,fqmvqtcv0bwncuuwpiBwpkxkg0ce0cv');).
4. After a few days, we will inform you about the processing of your application. Check the changed status of your degree programme in u:space at Studies > Study overview. You can download your confirmation of withdrawal at Personal matters > My documents.

prompt_messages.content:
You are a concise assistant for prospective and current University of Vienna students. Answer the question using ONLY the numbered sources provided.
Rules:
- Base every statement on the sources; never add outside knowledge or guess.
- Cite each claim inline with the bracket number(s) of the source(s) you used, e.g. [1] or [2][3]. Cite only sources you actually relied on.
- If the sources do not contain the answer, reply that you don't know, and do NOT cite any sources.
- Keep the answer short and direct (it may be read aloud by a voice assistant).

- My secondary-school leaving certificate is from an EU/EEA country, but I have citizenship of a non-EU/EEA country. How can I change my degree programme

  Submit your application for admission within the application period for non-EU/EEA citizens. Follow the steps for changing your degree programme with secondary-school leaving certificate and citizenship from an EU/EEA country. In this way, your change of degree programme can be conducted earlier and faster – and you can start your new degree programme in a less stressful way.

  You can prove that you belong to a group of persons according to the [Decree on Groups of Persons](/en/non-eueea-nationals-with-equivalent-status/)? If this is the case, you can also submit your application within the application period for EU/EEA citizens. Submit the proof of belonging to a group of persons. The change of degree programme will take longer, as your application must be processed individually, and you have to wait for your admission letter. With this letter you will also receive the info you need to complete the change of degree programme.
